context.meal: breaded fish bites, roasted potatoes, tartar sauce

episode_text:
day=20; meal=breaded fish bites, roasted potatoes, tartar sauce; setting=Personal; time_of_day=noon;
preferences: microwave_time=2 min; occlusion_relevance=do not consider occlusion; robot_speed=medium; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=outside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=far; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=open mouth; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=button; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=button; retract_between_bites=no; bite_dipping_preference=less; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=100 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF food is fruit or a cold dessert AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND dining context involves watching TV in front, watching TV on the right, working on a laptop on the left, working on a laptop in front, or social with a person on the left, THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose not to heat fruit-based meals, consistent with preserving intended cold serving temperature, across multiple attention and social contexts. IF food is a fruit/dessert meal with a dip or topping AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND dining context involves watching TV on the left, THEN 1 min BECAUSE observed left-TV dessert evidence shows the user may lightly warm some mixed dessert meals rather than keeping them fully cold. IF food is a warm savory entree with sauce already incorporated AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND dining context involves working on a laptop on the left, social with a person on the right, social with a person on the left, watching TV on the right, or a quiet personal setting, THEN 2 min is the main preference overall, but 1 min can occur in a quiet personal setting or for a warm savory meal with a separate dip/condiment in the social-right context BECAUSE repeated savory selections support 2 min as the baseline reheating choice, while some dippable meals show a lighter-reheat exception. IF food is a warm savory meal with separate components suitable for reheating AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND dining context involves working on a laptop on the right or watching TV in front, THEN 3 min BECAUSE the user selected stronger reheating for some warm savory meals in those contexts. IF food is a warm savory meal with a separate dip or condiment AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND dining context involves watching TV in front, THEN 1 min can occur as a lighter-reheat option BECAUSE the user previously selected lighter reheating for dippable savory meals in that context. IF food is a warm savory entree with sauce already incorporated AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND dining context involves working on a laptop on the left, THEN 1 min can occur as a lighter-reheat exception BECAUSE that choice has occurred before, though 2 min remains better supported overall. No broader affect-dependent variation observed yet.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE this has been selected repeatedly to avoid blocking a social partner on the right. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN minimize left occlusion is common but do not consider occlusion has also occurred BECAUSE partner-aware left-side visibility often matters there, though not uniformly. IF dining context involves watching TV in front or working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly preserved visibility toward a front-facing attentional target. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right or working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user consistently preferred to preserve visibility toward a right-side attentional target in those meals. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a cold fruit/dessert meal, THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the right-TV fruit meal showed that right-side attentional contexts do not always trigger occlusion minimization. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left, working on a laptop on the left, or a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly accepted no occlusion constraint in those nonsocial or left-side nonsocial contexts, and the new personal savory meal reinforces that private dining does not itself create an occlusion concern. Overall, social partner location and front-facing media/work are the strongest drivers of occlusion minimization.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN fast is common but medium can also occur BECAUSE earlier front-TV meals favored quicker interaction, while later front-TV evidence showed the user does not always want maximum speed in that context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a fruit/dessert meal, THEN fast BECAUSE observed left-TV dessert evidence shows the user may prioritize quick interaction in that media-focused context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory entree, THEN fast BECAUSE the user preferred a quicker pace while multitasking. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal with a separate dip/condiment, THEN both fast and medium have occurred, with medium now better supported overall in private savory meals BECAUSE one personal dippable meal favored shorter meal time, but the new personal fish-and-tartar meal shows the user also accepts a moderate pace in the same broad context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left, working on a laptop in front, social with a person on the right, social with a person on the left, watching TV on the right, watching TV on the left with other observed foods, or a quiet personal setting with other observed foods AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN medium BECAUSE the user usually retained a moderate pace in those contexts. No broader affect-dependent variation observed yet.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF dining context is any observed context AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user has consistently selected this option across meals, favoring bite acquisition reliability. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context is any observed context AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN no BECAUSE the user has consistently skipped explicit web confirmation of bite acquisition, indicating a stable preference to reduce interaction overhead. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV on the left, watching TV in front, working on a laptop on the left, working on a laptop on the right, or working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN inside mouth transfer is common BECAUSE the user usually chose direct delivery when attention was occupied, and observed left-TV dessert evidence reinforces that left-side media contexts can also favor convenience. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN outside mouth transfer was previously the dominant social-right pattern, but inside mouth transfer also occurs for a warm savory meal with a separate dip/condiment BECAUSE repeated social-right meals supported the less intrusive style overall, while one meal showed convenience can override that baseline. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN both inside mouth transfer and outside mouth transfer have been selected BECAUSE the user does not show a single fixed transfer style in that context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the observed right-TV savory meals supported the less intrusive, user-reachable style there. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a cold fruit/dessert meal, THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the right-TV fruit meal showed that in the same environmental context the user may switch to lower-effort direct delivery for lighter cold foods. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN outside mouth transfer is the better-supported baseline overall, but inside mouth transfer can occur for a warm savory meal with a separate dip/condiment BECAUSE personal-setting evidence mostly supports the less intrusive style, while one private dippable savory meal showed convenience can also matter. Across observed contexts, the user is physically capable of outside-mouth transfer but often prefers inside-mouth transfer when convenience or divided attention appears more salient.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context is social with a person on the right, social with a person on the left, or watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN medium BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected an outside-mouth distance that balances comfort and reachability in those settings. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal without a strong convenience cue, THEN medium is the baseline, but far can occur for a warm savory meal with a separate dip/condiment BECAUSE earlier private meals supported a balanced distance, while the new personal fish-and-tartar meal shows the user can prefer a more self-directed reach in private. IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer, THEN not applicable BECAUSE this parameter does not apply when the robot delivers food directly into the mouth.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN no cue was previously common but LED also occurs BECAUSE repeated front-TV meals initially showed a preference to remove readiness notifications, while later evidence shows the user may retain a lightweight visual cue rather than fully suppressing prompts. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a fruit/dessert meal, THEN no cue BECAUSE observed left-TV dessert evidence shows the user may suppress readiness prompts entirely in a media-focused context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory entree, THEN no cue BECAUSE the user suppressed readiness notifications while engaged in a concurrent task. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left or working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is cold fruit, THEN speech BECAUSE the user selected an explicit auditory cue for fruit meals in work contexts. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal with a separate dip/condiment, THEN both no cue and speech + LED have occurred BECAUSE one private dippable savory meal favored minimal interruption, while the new personal fish-and-tartar meal shows the user also accepts explicit multimodal prompting in private. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right or a quiet personal setting with other observed foods AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN speech + LED is the better-supported baseline BECAUSE the user selected the most explicit multimodal readiness cue in those contexts, and the new private savory meal reinforces that private dining can favor clear prompting. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal with a separate dip/condiment, THEN no cue BECAUSE that meal showed the user can prefer fully suppressed prompting in that context. IF dining context is social with a person on the right OR involves watching TV on the left with other observed foods OR watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any other observed food, THEN LED is common BECAUSE the user often retained a lightweight visual cue in those contexts rather than requiring multimodal prompting. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN both speech + LED and LED have been selected, with recent evidence favoring lighter cueing BECAUSE the user appears comfortable with reduced verbal prompting in social settings. Overall, social and attention-occupied contexts tend to reduce cue intensity relative to quiet personal settings.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN button was previously common, but autocontinue also occurs for a warm savory meal with a separate dip/condiment BECAUSE social settings can motivate explicit control to avoid confusion with talking, yet one meal showed the user may instead favor lower-effort automation. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN both open mouth and button have been selected BECAUSE conversational ambiguity can matter there, though the user has not shown a single fixed rule. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user preferred a low-effort but still user-driven initiation method in that right-TV savory context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a cold fruit/dessert meal, THEN button BECAUSE the right-TV fruit meal showed the user can shift back to explicit initiation in the same environmental context, so food type matters there. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a fruit/dessert meal, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE observed left-TV dessert evidence shows the user may favor maximum automation in that media-focused context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is other observed foods, THEN button BECAUSE earlier left-TV evidence supported explicit initiation there. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN autocontinue was previously common but button also occurs BECAUSE earlier front-TV meals favored fully automatic initiation, while later front-TV evidence showed the user can shift back to explicit control. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory entree, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automatic initiation while multitasking. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left or working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is cold fruit, THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user shifted to a low-effort but still user-driven initiation method for fruit meals in work contexts. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user again preferred low-effort embodied initiation rather than button press or full automation. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN open mouth is now the better-supported baseline overall, but autocontinue can occur for a warm savory meal with a separate dip/condiment BECAUSE private-setting evidence otherwise supports low-effort user-driven initiation, while one personal dippable savory meal showed a shift toward maximum automation.

IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal with a separate dip/condiment, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE that meal showed the user can prefer lower-effort automatic drink initiation in that context. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal with a separate dip/condiment, THEN both autocontinue and button have occurred BECAUSE one private dippable savory meal favored automatic drink initiation, while the new personal fish-and-tartar meal shows the user also often prefers explicit control in private. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a fruit/dessert meal, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE observed left-TV dessert evidence shows the user may also favor automatic drink initiation in that media-focused context. IF dining context is social with a person on the right, social with a person on the left, watching TV on the right, watching TV on the left with other observed foods, working on a laptop on the left, working on a laptop in front, or a quiet personal setting with other observed foods AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any other observed food, THEN button BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose explicit signaling for drink initiation in those contexts, and the new private savory meal reinforces button as the private baseline. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN autocontinue was previously common but button also occurs BECAUSE repeated front-TV meals had shown a preference for automatic drink initiation, while later evidence demonstrates that explicit control can still be preferred there. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user selected a lower-effort but still user-driven initiation method in that right-work context.

IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal with a separate dip/condiment, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE that meal showed the user can prefer lower-effort automatic wiping initiation in that context. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal with a separate dip/condiment, THEN both autocontinue and button have occurred BECAUSE one private dippable savory meal favored automatic wiping initiation, while the new personal fish-and-tartar meal shows the user also often prefers explicit control in private. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a fruit/dessert meal, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE observed left-TV dessert evidence shows the user may also favor automatic wiping initiation in that media-focused context. IF dining context is social with a person on the right, social with a person on the left, watching TV on the right, watching TV on the left with other observed foods, working on a laptop on the left, working on a laptop in front, or a quiet personal setting with other observed foods AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any other observed food, THEN button BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose explicit signaling for mouth-wiping initiation in those contexts, and the new private savory meal reinforces button as the private baseline. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN autocontinue was previously common but button also occurs BECAUSE repeated front-TV meals had shown a preference for automatic wiping initiation, while later evidence demonstrates that explicit control can still be preferred there. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user selected a lower-effort but still user-driven initiation method in that right-work context.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN no cue was previously common but LED also occurs BECAUSE repeated front-TV meals initially showed a preference to suppress completion notifications, while later evidence shows the user may instead keep a lightweight visual completion cue. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a fruit/dessert meal, THEN no cue BECAUSE observed left-TV dessert evidence shows the user may suppress completion prompts entirely in a media-focused context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory entree, THEN no cue BECAUSE the user suppressed completion notifications while engaged in a concurrent task. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left or working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is cold fruit, THEN speech BECAUSE the user selected an auditory completion cue for fruit meals in work contexts. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal with a separate dip/condiment, THEN both no cue and speech + LED have occurred BECAUSE one private dippable savory meal favored minimal interruption, while the new personal fish-and-tartar meal shows the user also accepts explicit multimodal completion prompting in private. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right or a quiet personal setting with other observed foods AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN speech + LED is the better-supported baseline BECAUSE the user selected the most explicit multimodal completion cue in those contexts, and the new private savory meal reinforces that private dining can favor clear prompting. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal with a separate dip/condiment, THEN no cue BECAUSE that meal showed the user can prefer fully suppressed completion prompting in that context. IF dining context is social with a person on the right OR involves watching TV on the left with other observed foods OR watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any other observed food, THEN LED is common BECAUSE the user often retained a lightweight visual completion cue in those contexts. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN both speech + LED and LED have been selected, with recent evidence favoring lighter cueing BECAUSE the user appears comfortable with reduced verbal prompting in social settings. Overall, social and attention-occupied contexts tend to reduce cue intensity relative to quiet personal settings.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right, social with a person on the left, watching TV on the right, watching TV in front, watching TV on the left, working on a laptop on the left, working on a laptop on the right, working on a laptop in front, or a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently preferred reliable automatic detection for bite completion rather than requiring extra manual input across all observed contexts, and the new personal savory meal reinforces that stability.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a fruit/dessert meal, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE observed left-TV dessert evidence shows the user may prefer fully automatic drink completion in that media-focused context. IF dining context is social with a person on the right, social with a person on the left, watching TV on the right, watching TV on the left with other observed foods, working on a laptop on the left, working on a laptop in front, watching TV in front, or a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any other observed food, THEN button is the better-supported choice overall BECAUSE the accumulated evidence shows a repeated preference for explicit completion signaling for drinking in many contexts, and the new private savory meal reinforces that private baseline. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE prior right-work evidence indicates that when attention is strongly occupied in that context, reduced interaction burden can override explicit control.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a fruit/dessert meal, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE observed left-TV dessert evidence shows the user may prefer fully automatic mouth-wiping completion in that media-focused context. IF dining context is social with a person on the right, social with a person on the left, watching TV on the right, watching TV on the left with other observed foods, working on a laptop on the left, working on a laptop in front, watching TV in front, or a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any other observed food, THEN button is the better-supported choice overall BECAUSE the accumulated evidence shows a repeated preference for explicit completion signaling for mouth wiping in many contexts, and the new private savory meal reinforces that private baseline. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE prior right-work evidence indicates that when attention is occupied, the user can prefer lower-effort automatic progression.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN yes was previously common, but no also occurs for a warm savory meal with a separate dip/condiment BECAUSE repeated social-right meals indicated a stable preference to reduce prolonged visual obstruction, while one meal showed that the user may instead prioritize faster, less interrupted interaction. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN both no and yes have been selected BECAUSE the user does not show a single fixed social-left rule but can accept longer interaction to reduce obstruction. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected retracting in a right-work context, consistent with protecting visibility toward a right-side task. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN no BECAUSE the observed right-TV savory meals supported prioritizing faster, less interrupted interaction there. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a cold fruit/dessert meal, THEN yes BECAUSE the right-TV fruit meal showed that in the same environmental context the user may accept longer interaction to reduce obstruction for lighter cold foods. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN no was previously common but yes also occurs BECAUSE earlier front-TV meals favored not retracting, while later front-TV evidence showed the user may accept longer interaction to reduce obstruction in that same context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN no BECAUSE repeated left-TV meals show the user prefers not to retract there. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory entree, THEN no BECAUSE the user prioritized faster, less interrupted interaction while working. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left or working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is cold fruit, THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected retracting for fruit meals in work contexts, indicating that work contexts do not uniformly suppress retraction and that lighter foods can coincide with greater willingness to accept longer interaction for less obstruction. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN no BECAUSE personal-setting evidence supports faster, less interrupted interaction as the baseline when no social or attentional visibility concern is present, and the new private savory meal reinforces that pattern.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food includes a separate dip or sauce AND dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like, THEN less can occur, but do not dip also occurs BECAUSE the user previously preferred some dipping while limiting messiness in a social-right context, while another social-right meal showed that even with ranch available the user may choose to avoid dipping entirely. IF food includes a topping or dippable accompaniment associated with the bite AND dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like, THEN less can occur, but do not dip also occurs for savory meals without a selected dip BECAUSE an earlier strawberries-with-whipped-cream meal showed some topping use in social-left dining, while a later savory social-left meal supported no dipping when no dip is desired. IF food includes a separate dip or condiment AND dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like, THEN less and do not dip have both occurred, with less now better supported for private savory meals with a salient dip BECAUSE an earlier personal meal with ketchup and the new personal fish-and-tartar meal both show willingness to use some dip in private, while another private ranch meal shows the user may still avoid dipping entirely. IF food includes a dessert sauce or sweet dip AND dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like, THEN do not dip BECAUSE observed bananas/brownies/chocolate-sauce evidence shows the user may avoid dipping even when a sweet sauce is available in that media-focused context. IF food has no dippable item or sauce, THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is not applicable. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory entree with sauce already incorporated rather than a separate dip, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user does not treat incorporated sauce as a cue to dip. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right, watching TV in front, watching TV on the left, working on a laptop on the left, working on a laptop on the right, or working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is food with sauce already incorporated, with a separate condiment/dip, or fruit without dip, THEN do not dip BECAUSE repeated selections show the user generally avoids dipping in multitasking or media-focused nonsocial contexts. Overall, social settings can support less when a dip or topping is salient, but private savory meals with a clear dip also often support less rather than more.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a fruit/dessert meal, THEN 10 sec can occur, but 1000 sec has also been selected in that environmental context BECAUSE observed left-TV dessert evidence shows the user may sometimes prefer rapid automatic progression there, while earlier left-TV evidence supported very long intervention time, so this context remains variable. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the observed right-TV savory meals support a moderate wait rather than maximal delay. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a cold fruit/dessert meal, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the right-TV fruit meal showed the user may want much more intervention time for lighter cold foods in that same context. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN 10 sec was previously common but 1000 sec also occurs BECAUSE earlier front-TV meals showed a preference for rapid automatic progression, while later front-TV evidence showed the user can instead want very long intervention time in that same context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory entree, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected a very short wait while multitasking. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left or working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is cold fruit, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user selected a very long wait for fruit meals in work contexts, showing that lighter foods can shift work contexts toward preserving ample intervention time. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected a short wait in that right-work context, favoring quick automatic progression. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal with a separate dip/condiment, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE that meal showed that the user can prefer rapid automatic progression in that context. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any other observed food, THEN 100 sec BECAUSE earlier social-right evidence supported a moderate wait that balances explicit control with enough time to respond. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN both 10 sec and 100 sec have been selected, with 100 sec somewhat better supported BECAUSE the user does not show a single fixed wait policy there. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN 100 sec is the better-supported baseline overall, but 10 sec can occur for a warm savory meal with a separate dip/condiment BECAUSE private-setting evidence now favors a moderate wait in general, while one personal dippable savory meal showed a preference for rapid automatic progression.
